But Christian, can I say tonight on this Sunday night, it's time that we get over whatever our hindrance is and simply obey Christ's commands. Yes, it may be a little bit scary. It may push you out of your comfort zone, but obey Christ's commands. And I promise you, whenever you do, you have the Lord working with you and God will give you the strength to do it. And God can do some incredible things through your life. There was a recent poll taken. It was a Barna poll. So it's a pretty reputable poll. And they ask people a bunch of questions in this certain survey. It was this kind of a spiritual survey, if you will. And they ask a bunch of questions. And one of the questions that they asked was, do you believe that the Bible way to heaven is by grace through faith? And by the way, that is the Bible way to heaven, by grace through faith, not of our works. In that survey, only 6% of people, this is a survey taken here in America, only 6% of people in America said that that was the Bible way to heaven, 6%. Here in America, where we got churches everywhere, 6% of people, less than one in 10 people in America said that that was the Bible way to heaven. So that kind of got me thinking a little bit. So I did a Google search. I said, how many people a day in the United States die? What a Google search, right? And I was like, how many people in the United States die a day? And in a rough Google search, you'll get a few varying numbers, but generally speaking, people will have it around 8,400 people, roughly speaking. The one that I got right here is 8,460 people each day die in the United States. So if we take, we'll round up to 10%. Let's just, for sake of illustration tonight, say that 10% of people in America are saved, which again, I know that poll could be skewed, it could be up or down, I understand that. Let's just say 10% of people in America are saved, with 8,460 people each day dying. If you do the math on that, if that stat is true, 508 people each day who die here in America are saved and go into heaven. But if that stat is true, 7,952 people each day who die are lost and going to spend an eternity in a godless place called hell. Let that number sink in. Nearly, here in America, we're not talking the world, we're talking America. Nearly 8,000 people each day who die are going to spend to a godless place called hell. That ought to break our hearts as Christians. To think that that many people are dying each and every day, and they don't know Jesus as their Savior. If that doesn't motivate you to share the gospel, friend, can I say this? I don't know what will. It's time that we as Christians take Christ's command seriously to go into all the world to preach the gospel to every creature. Oh, that we would have a church here at Fellowship Baptist Church that takes the gospel seriously. That goes out and shares the gospel with their friends, with their family, with their co-workers, with everybody they come in contact with. The best message that anybody could hear, the message of the gospel. I'll close with one more illustration. I was recently coming back on the metro one evening. And to be honest with you, I was a little bit tired. I was exhausted from the long day out. And I was sitting there at the metro. I was probably playing on my phone, maybe looking at some sports highlights or something like that. And there was a man that got on the metro. He looked a little bit disheveled. And he could tell he was a little bit down and out. But kind of as he got in, I didn't think anything of it. And kind of the Holy Spirit just started pricking my heart. Hey, talk to him about the Lord. Hey, give him a tract. And I was like, Lord, I really don't feel like it right now. I'm tired. I just want to go back home. But the Holy Spirit was like, hey, hey, talk to him. So I was like, yes, sir, Lord. So I got up and I had a gospel tract on me. And I said, hey, sir, could I give you something that talks about God's love for you? Whatever I said, I'm not exactly sure. And that man looked up at me. And as sincere as he could, he said, huh. He said, I didn't grow up Christian. I didn't grow up going to church. But recently I've been thinking about Christianity and about God. Whoa. I was not expecting that response. To be honest with you, I was expecting him to say, no, I'm good. Thank you. Go on, man. But he was open to the gospel. Could there be some people like that in your lives? That God might just put them right in front of you that are looking for something different? I can promise you that, yes, there's pleasure and sin for a season. But the world's philosophies and ways, they're not going to do anything for a person. Only the gospel and a personal relationship with Jesus can bring hope and peace to a person's life. Could God put some people in your life this week, tomorrow at work, that you're able to share the gospel with? Why not pray about it? Christian, are you sharing the gospel? Do you have a burden for the lost? Why not tonight make a choice that you're going to be a gospel Christian and share the gospel? If you would bow your heads and close your eyes, I'm going to ask Pastor Mann to come. I'm going to pray here in a second and then let him take the invitation, however the Lord leads. I thank you just for dying on the cross, Lord, to pay for my sins, to give us the message of the gospel. God, I pray that you help each one of us to be gospel Christians and to simply go and share the gospel. Lord, take control of the invitation. It's yours. In Jesus' name we pray.
